rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK FKA GREENWOOD TRUST COMPANY VS. CHRISTOPHER S RECTOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 4, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DISCOVER BANK FKA GREENWOOD TRUST COMPANY's Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LIGHTFOOT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Ccp Sec 128.7 Motion For Sanctions; Memo Of P&A; Request For Judicail Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 4, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD (CALTRAIN) Notice Of Motion And Motion Under CCP Sec 128.7 Motion For Sanctions. Granted. As sanctions for violation of Code of Civil Procedure 128.7(b), this action is dismissed as against the moving party and the moving party is awarded $2,000 as reasonable fees for bringing this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 128.7(e) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY, A VS. TEXAS DEVELOPMENT, INC., A NEVADA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel The Continued Deposition Of The Person Most Knowledeable For Texas Development Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 4, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel The Continued Deposition Of The Person Most Knowledeable For Texas Development Inc. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED. Section 362(a) of the Bankruptcy Code does not preclude generation of information regarding claims by or against a non-debtor party, even where that information could eventually adversely affect the Debtor. Miller v. Miller (9th Cir. B.A.P. 2001) 262 B.R. 499, 505 (holding that party who moved to compel deposition of Debtor in action against Debtor's husband did not violate the automatic stay). This ruling is limited to the propriety of moving to compel and taking the deposition of Mr. Cook. It does not purport to rule on the question of whether certain topics of questioning during the deposition could potentially violate the automatic stay created by Mr. Cook's petition in bankruptcy given that the question presented to the Bankruptcy Appellate Panel in the Miller case was, "does the automatic stay protect a debtor from complying with discovery requests in a multi-defendant case where the debtor is a defendant, but where those discovery requests are framed as discovery pertaining only to the claims against the other non-debtor defendants?" (emphasis added). The request for sanctions is DENIED. Judge Pro Tem Halperin discloses that, within the past 16 months I represented a party in a civil proceeding who was adverse to a party represented by the Steyer Lowenthal firm. I do not believe that representation requires my recusal from this matter. I encourage any party who has concerns regarding this disclosure to discuss them at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE BATLIN VS. LANGE TRUCKING INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Production Of Documents And Req. For Appointment Of Referee; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 4, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANTs LANGE TRUCKING INC. AND JOHN HOWARD RODGERS (SUED AS JOHN HOWARD RODGERT)'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Request For Appointment Of Referee. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel production of documents and request for appointment of a referee is GRANTED IN PART. In this personal injury action resulting from a motor vehicle action, plaintiff claims traumatic brain injury. The declaration of David Seidenwurm, M.D. establishes that plaintiff's medical records subpoenaed by Defendants is necessary in order for him to reach a conclusion regarding the etiology of the MRI findings and their relationship to clinical symptoms, and thus offer an opinion as to causality, and will be helpful to the anticipated neuropsychological examination of Plaintiff. Defendants' request for appointment of a discovery referee is GRANTED ON THE CONDITION that Defendants bear the entire cost of the referee. Within 5 days Plaintiff and Defendants are to submit to the court the names of three persons agreeable to all parties in this litigation, and the court will appoint one of them. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ADAM SHRYOCK et al VS. KELLI GAZICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 4, 2012, Line 8. CROSS DEFENDANT ADAM SHRYOCK MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied. On a motion to strike, the Court may only consider the pleadings and matters which can be judicially noticed. The parties' contract is not subject to judicial notice. The allegations of paragraphs 35 and 75 of the complaint appear to have some nexus to Ms. Gazich's claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. The request to strike these paragraphs appears to be a disguised motion in limine or motion for protective order and plaintiff's concerns are better addressed in the context of those motions, not a motion to strike. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
